mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite unemployment deterioration, or Spearman rank correlation between UNRATE change and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1.2 million units annualized, indicating weakening aggregate demand. This joint deterioration in consumption and investment signals reduced corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth < 2% annualized AND HOUST < 1.2M units annualized
regime_hint: demand-constrained environment with synchronized weakness in consumption and investment
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_20d on RSAFS and HOUST interaction.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) exhibits a month-over-month decline of more than 0.5% while remaining below its 12-month moving average, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflationary pressures. This supply contraction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth and inflation expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% MoM AND INDPRO < 12-month MA
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite supply contraction, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100,000 jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable or rises, indicating a labor market inflection point. This signal of slowing job creation produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate earnings pressure from reduced consumer income growth.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100K AND UNRATE stable or rising
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market deterioration with employment growth deceleration
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE change is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting direction_20d.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, signaling a shift toward below-trend economic expansion. This demand-side slowdown produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings growth expectations are revised downward and recession risk premiums increase.
regime_hint: below-trend growth environment with deceleration risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 5.0%, indicating real rates are insufficiently restrictive. This monetary policy lag produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate future rate hikes and earnings margin compression from persistent inflation.
regime_hint: inflation-persistent environment with monetary policy lag
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term between inflation and real rates is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_20d.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) trend upward above 1.4 million units annualized while real retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously grow above 3% annualized, indicating robust aggregate demand and forward-looking economic strength. This synchronized demand expansion produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors increase equity allocations on improved growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1.4M units annualized AND RSAFS growth > 3% annualized
regime_hint: demand-robust expansion with synchronized strength in consumption and investment
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_5d on HOUST and RSAFS interaction.
